title: Enchiladas Verdes
description: A Mexican comfort classic. Soft corn tortillas are stuffed with shredded chicken and drenched in a vibrant, tangy salsa verde made from roasted tomatillos and serrano chilies. Topped with crumbled queso fresco and a drizzle of crema.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Tomatillos","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"husked and washed"},{"name":"Serrano Chilies","qty":"2","unit":"pcs","notes":"for heat"},{"name":"Shredded Chicken","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"poached and pulled"},{"name":"Corn Tortillas","qty":"12","unit":"pcs","notes":"freshly made"},{"name":"Queso Fresco","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"crumbled"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Salsa Verde**: Roast tomatillos, onions, and chilies until charred. Blend with cilantro, garlic, and a splash of chicken stock. Simmer the sauce for 10 minutes to thicken and deepen the flavor.
2. **The Tortillas**: Lightly fry the tortillas in oil for 5 seconds per side—this stops them from falling apart when soaked in sauce.
3. **Assembly**: Dip a tortilla in the hot salsa, fill with chicken, and roll. Arrange in a dish, cover with more salsa, and garnish with crema, queso fresco, and thin onion rings.

title: Chiles Rellenos
description: Large, mild Poblano chilies are roasted, peeled, and stuffed with melty Oaxaca cheese. They are then dipped in a light, fluffy egg batter and fried until golden. A true testament to the layers of Mexican culinary technique.
stepsMd:
1. **The Roast**: Char the peppers over an open flame until the skin is black. Place in a bag to steam for 10 minutes, then peel gently. Make a small slit and remove seeds carefully.
2. **The Stuffing**: Stuff each pepper with cheese. Dust the outside with flour—this helps the egg batter stick.
3. **The Batter**: Beat egg whites to stiff peaks, then gently fold in the yolks. Dip peppers in the batter and shallow fry until golden and puffed. Serve with a simple warm tomato broth.

title: Ratatouille (Confit Byaldi)
description: The elegant, thinly-sliced version of the Provençal vegetable stew. Layers of summer squash, eggplant, and tomato are slow-roasted over a rich 'piperade' base of peppers and onions. A vegan masterpiece of precision and patience.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Eggplant, Zucchini, Yellow Squash","qty":"2","unit":"each","notes":"uniformly sized for slicing"},{"name":"Roma Tomatoes","qty":"6","unit":"pcs","notes":"firm"},{"name":"Bell Peppers","qty":"2","unit":"pcs","notes":"for the piperade base"},{"name":"Herbes de Provence","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"fragrant dried herbs"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Piperade**: Sauté onions, garlic, and peppers until soft and caramelized. Puree until smooth and spread as a foundation in a round baking dish.
2. **The Spiral**: Use a mandoline to slice the vegetables into paper-thin disks. Arrange them in a tight, overlapping spiral over the piperade. Drizzle with olive oil and spices.
3. **The Slow Roast**: Cover with parchment paper and bake at 150°C for 90 minutes. The vegetables should be tender but hold their shape. The flavor intensifies as it rests.

title: Coq au Vin
description: The definitive French bistro classic. Chicken is braised slowly in a bottle of full-bodied red wine (traditionally Burgundy), enriched with smoky bacon lardons, pearl onions, and earthy button mushrooms.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Whole Chicken","qty":"1.5","unit":"kg","notes":"cut into joints"},{"name":"Red Wine (Pinot Noir)","qty":"750","unit":"ml","notes":"the better the wine, the better the dish"},{"name":"Bacon Lardons","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"for smoky depth"},{"name":"Pearl Onions","qty":"15","unit":"pcs","notes":"peeled whole"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Sear**: Fry the bacon until crispy. Remove, then sear the chicken joints in the bacon fat until deeply browned. Remove. Sauté mirepoix (onions, carrots) in the same fat.
2. **The Braise**: Add wine, chicken stock, and a bouquet garni. Return the chicken to the pot. Cover and simmer for 2 hours until the wine reduces to a dark, purple nectar.
3. **The Texture**: In a separate pan, sauté mushrooms and pearl onions until golden. Add them to the pot in the last 15 minutes. Thicken the sauce with a 'beurre manié' (butter and flour paste) if needed.
cookwareJson: ["Heavy Dutch oven", "Frying pan", "Kitchen twine for herbs"]

title: Moussaka
description: Greece's answer to lasagna. Layers of pan-seared eggplant and spiced ground lamb ragù, topped with a thick, custard-like blanket of Béchamel that is baked until golden and bubbling.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Eggplant","qty":"3","unit":"large","notes":"sliced into rounds"},{"name":"Ground Lamb","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"sweet and savory"},{"name":"Cinnamon & Allspice","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"for the signature Greek aroma"},{"name":"Béchamel Sauce","qty":"500","unit":"ml","notes":"with a touch of nutmeg and egg yolk"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Prep**: Salt the eggplant slices to draw out moisture, then fry or roast until golden. This prevents the dish from being soggy.
2. **The Ragù**: Sauté onions and lamb until browned. Add tomato puree, red wine, and warming spices (cinnamon is key). Simmer until the sauce is thick and almost dry.
3. **The Custard Layer**: Layer eggplant and meat sauce in a dish. Pour over a thick Béchamel enriched with egg yolks and grated Kefalotyri cheese. Bake at 180°C for 45 minutes until the top is beautifully brûléed.

title: Spanakopita
description: Flaky, buttery filo pastry stuffed with a vibrant mixture of spinach, salty feta, and fresh dill. This Greek pie is a masterclass in crisp textures and herbal brightness.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Fresh Spinach","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"wilted and squeezed dry"},{"name":"Feta Cheese","qty":"250","unit":"g","notes":"crumbled"},{"name":"Filo Pastry","qty":"1","unit":"pack","notes":"thawed carefully"},{"name":"Fresh Dill & Mint","qty":"1","unit":"bunch","notes":"finely chopped"},{"name":"Butter","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"melted for layering"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Filling**: Mix the dry, chopped spinach with feta, herbs, and eggs. The spinach must be COMPLETELY dry to keep the pastry crisp.
2. **The Filo Ritual**: Brush each sheet of filo with melted butter. Layer 6-8 sheets in a pan. Add the spinach filling, then top with another 6-8 sheets of buttered filo.
3. **The Diamond Cut**: Score the top layers of pastry into diamonds before baking—this makes serving easy and adds a professional touch. Bake until the pastry is golden-brown and shattering to the touch.

title: Kung Pao Chicken
description: A Sichuan stir-fry classic that balances the 'mala' (numbing and spicy) sensation with a glossy sweet-and-sour glaze. Featuring crispy chicken, scorched dried chilies, and crunchy peanuts.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Chicken Breast","qty":"400","unit":"g","notes":"cut into cubes"},{"name":"Sichuan Peppercorns","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"for numbing effect"},{"name":"Dried Red Chilies","qty":"10","unit":"pcs","notes":"deseeded"},{"name":"Peanuts","qty":"50","unit":"g","notes":"roasted and unsalted"},{"name":"Chinkiang Vinegar","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"black vinegar"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Velveting**: Marinate chicken with cornstarch, soy sauce, and a splash of Shaoxing wine for 20 minutes. This creates a protective 'velvet' coating that keeps the meat moist.
2. **The Scorch**: Heat oil in a wok until smoking. Fry peppercorns and chilies until they turn dark brown—the oil is now 'infused' with the Sichuan soul.
3. **The Stir-Fry**: Sear chicken over maximum heat. Add aromatics (garlic, ginger, scallions). Pour in the pre-mixed sauce (vinegar, soy, sugar, starch). Toss with peanuts at the last second to keep them crunchy.
cookwareJson: ["Carbon steel wok", "Long-handled wok spatula", "Small bowl for sauce mixing"]

title: Mapo Tofu
description: Perhaps the most iconic dish from Sichuan. Soft, silky tofu cubes are bathed in a rich, red, numbing chili-bean sauce (doubanjiang), accented with minced pork and a heavy dusting of Sichuan pepper.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Silken Tofu","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"cut into cubes"},{"name":"Minced Pork","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"can be swapped for mushrooms"},{"name":"Doubanjiang","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"fermented chili bean paste"},{"name":"Dashi or Chicken Stock","qty":"200","unit":"ml","notes":"for the sauce base"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Tofu Bath**: Gently simmer the tofu cubes in salted boiling water for 2 minutes. This firms them up so they don't break in the wok.
2. **The Base**: Fry minced pork until crispy. Add doubanjiang and cook until the oil turns a bright, vibrant red. This 'releasing of the color' is crucial for the dish's aesthetic.
3. **The Marriage**: Add stock and tofu. Simmer for 5 minutes. Thicken with a cornstarch slurry until the sauce clings to every cube. Finish with a 'rain' of freshly ground Sichuan peppercorn and scallions.

title: Dim Sum: Siu Mai
description: Open-faced steamed dumplings from the tea houses of Guangzhou. A juicy mixture of pork and shrimp wrapped in a thin egg-skin, topped with a bright orange dot of crab roe or carrot.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Minced Pork Belly","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"high fat content for juiciness"},{"name":"Whole Shrimp","qty":"150","unit":"g","notes":"roughly chopped"},{"name":"Wonton Wrappers","qty":"20","unit":"pcs","notes":"round egg-based sheets"},{"name":"Shiitake Mushrooms","qty":"3","unit":"pcs","notes":"finely minced"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Filling**: Mix pork, shrimp, and mushrooms. Season with white pepper, sesame oil, and a pinch of sugar. Whisk the mixture in one direction until it becomes 'bouncy'—the protein must cross-link for the right texture.
2. **The Pleat**: Place filling in the center of a wrapper. Bring the sides up to form a cylinder but leave the top open. Squeeze gently to flatten the top.
3. **The Steam**: Line a bamboo steamer with parchment paper or cabbage leaves. Steam over boiling water for 8-10 minutes until the filling is opaque and the wrapper is translucent.
cookwareJson: ["Bamboo steamer", "Large wok or pot (for boiling water)", "Parchment paper"]

title: Paella Valenciana
description: The pride of coastal Spain. Bomba rice is cooked in a flat, wide pan (paellera) to create the legendary 'socarrat'—the caramelized, crispy crust on the bottom. Studded with seafood, saffron, and pimentón.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Bomba Rice","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"absorbs 3x its volume in stock"},{"name":"Mixed Seafood","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"Shrimp, mussels, calamari"},{"name":"Saffron Threads","qty":"1","unit":"pinch","notes":"soaked in warm water"},{"name":"Smoked Paprika (Pimentón)","qty":"1","unit":"tsp","notes":"for smoky depth"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Sofregit**: Sauté onions, garlic, and tomato in a paella pan until they form a thick, dark jam. This is the flavor engine.
2. **The Rice**: Add the rice and toast briefly. Pour in the saffron-infused stock. DO NOT STIR after this point—the rice must remain still to develop the crust.
3. **The Crust (Socarrat)**: Arrange the seafood on top. In the last 2 minutes, turn the heat up high. You should hear a faint 'crackle'—that's the rice caramelizing against the pan. Garnish with lemon wedges and fresh parsley.
